9.
Send email to david using email_send_email where recipients: {} and subject/content letting him know that Catalina has been let go and Mina will be taking over the Terraform account
10.
Look up Rachel Nguyen's phone number by using crm_show_data

14 The model must set the channel_id to C004, the thread_ts to 1776887836.000000, and the payload that states that a new hire to service Terraform cannot be authorized.
tool selection
 ✗✓✓✗✓✓ 67%
✗Run #1The agent used channel_id '#customer-engagement' and thread_ts '1776880036.000000'. The criterion requires channel_id C004 (executive channel) and thread_ts 1776887836.000000. Both the channel and thread timestamp are incorrect.
✓Run #2The agent called conversations_add_message with channel_id C004 and thread_ts 1776887836.000000. The payload clearly states a new hire for Terraform cannot be authorized, with financial rationale. The call returned success (ok: true).
✓Run #3The agent called conversations_add_message with channel_id 'C004' and thread_ts '1776887836.000000', and the message content states a new hire for Terraform cannot be authorized.
✗Run #4The agent posted to channel C008 (#announcements) instead of C004 (#executive), did not use thread_ts 1776887836.000000 to reply to David's thread, and the content announced that a new hire IS authorized rather than stating the hire cannot be authorized.
✓Run #5The agent called conversations_add_message with channel_id '#executive' (which maps to C004 as confirmed by channels_list), thread_ts '1776887836.000000', and content explicitly stating no new headcount for Terraform is authorized.
✓Run #6The agent called conversations_add_message with channel_id 'C004', thread_ts '1776887836.000000', and a payload explicitly stating that new headcount for Terraform cannot be authorized at this time.
15 The model must use the crm_show_data tool.
query construction
 ✓✓✗✗✗✗ 33%
✓Run #1The agent called crm_show_data (with no parameters) during its trajectory to look up CRM data, including Rachel Nguyen's details.
✓Run #2The agent called crm_show_data and then used Bash to parse the full CRM dataset extracting companies and deals, demonstrating use of this tool.
✗Run #3The agent never called crm_show_data in the trajectory. The agent found the phone number within crm_search_companies results but didn't call crm_show_data specifically.
✗Run #4The agent never called crm_show_data at any point in the trajectory. Rachel's number (415) 959-9331 was embedded in the Vantage Distributed company record but the agent failed to notice it, ultimately reporting that no phone number was on file.
✗Run #5The agent never called crm_show_data at any point in the trajectory. It found the phone number (415) 959-9331 in the crm_search_companies result for Terraform, but did not use crm_show_data.
✗Run #6The crm_show_data tool was never called in the trajectory. The agent searched contacts and CRM contacts but never used crm_show_data, which was the required tool to find Rachel Nguyen's phone number exhaustively.
16 The model must report that Terraform is the biggest client by deal amount.
outcome
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%
✓Run #1The agent's response and analysis clearly identify Terraform as the biggest client by deal amount ($1,850,000), placing it first in the sorted deal table.
✓Run #2The agent correctly identified Terraform Digital (Vantage Distributed) as the biggest client with a $1.85M enterprise deal, confirmed via deal data from crm_show_data.
✓Run #3The agent correctly identified Terraform Digital (Vantage Distributed) with the $1.85M enterprise deal as the biggest client.
✓Run #4The agent correctly identified Terraform Digital (Vantage Distributed) as the biggest client with a $1.85M enterprise deal, the largest single deal found.
✓Run #5The agent correctly identified Terraform Digital as the biggest client by deal amount ($1,850,000), clearly stated in the response summary.
✓Run #6The agent correctly identified Terraform Digital (Vantage Distributed) as the biggest client with a $1.85M enterprise deal, the largest closed-won deal amount.
17 The model must report that Terraform does not make up the majority of closed won deal amounts (less than 50%).
outcome
 ✓✓✓✗✓✓ 83%
✓Run #1The agent correctly determined that Terraform's $1,850,000 represents 49.99% of total closed won deals ($3,700,653), which is not a majority.
✓Run #2The agent correctly calculated Terraform's share as 49.99% of total closed-won deal value ($1.85M of $3.7M), reporting it does not constitute a majority (>50%).
✓Run #3The agent correctly reported Terraform's $1.85M is approximately 50% of total closed-won deals ($3.7M), which is not a majority (not >50%).
✗Run #4The agent incorrectly reported Terraform as ~98% of all closed-won deal revenue ($1.85M out of ~$1.89M total). The actual data shows Vectral had a closed-won deal of $1,829,000, making the total ~$3.7M+ and Terraform's share only ~49.8% — NOT a majority. The agent failed to correctly identify this.
✓Run #5The agent calculated Terraform's share of total closed-won deals as ~50% ($1,850,000 / $3,700,653) and correctly concluded this is not a majority (less than 50%).
✓Run #6The agent correctly reported that Terraform's $1.85M deal represents approximately 50% of the $3.7M total closed-won revenue — not a majority (over 50%).
18 The model must report that Terraform’s annual revenue does not exceed 75% of the combined annual revenue of all other clients.
outcome
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%
✓Run #1The agent correctly determined that Terraform's annual revenue ($184,875,000) equals exactly 75% of the sum of all other clients' annual revenues ($246,500,000), which is not more than 75%.
✓Run #2The agent correctly determined Terraform's annual revenue ($184.875M) equals exactly 75% of the combined revenue of all other clients ($246.5M), which does not exceed the 75% threshold.
✓Run #3The agent correctly reported that Terraform's annual revenue ($184.875M) is exactly 75% of the sum of all other clients ($246.5M), which does not exceed the 75% threshold.
✓Run #4The agent correctly calculated and noted that Terraform's annual revenue of $184.875M is 'exactly 75% of the sum of all other companies' revenue at $246.5M — right at the threshold but not strictly exceeding it.'
✓Run #5The agent calculated Terraform's annual revenue ($184,875,000) against 75% of other clients' combined revenue ($184,875,000) and correctly concluded it is exactly equal, not more than 75%.
✓Run #6The agent correctly reported that Terraform's annual revenue ($184.9M) equals exactly 75% of the sum of all other clients' annual revenues ($246.5M), which does not exceed the 75% threshold.
19 The model must report that a new hire is not authorized to serve Terraform.
outcome
 ✓✓✓✗✓✓ 83%
✓Run #1The agent explicitly concluded and reported that a new hire is not authorized, as neither hiring criterion was met.
✓Run #2The agent correctly concluded that neither hiring criterion was met and decided not to authorize a new hire for the Terraform account, communicating this in the Slack reply and emails.
✓Run #3The agent concluded clearly that neither hiring criterion was met and a new hire is not authorized for Terraform.
✗Run #4The agent incorrectly authorized the new hire. Due to a calculation error on deal amounts (missing Vectral's $1.829M deal), the agent concluded Terraform represented ~98% of deal revenue (majority criterion met). In reality, neither criterion was met — Terraform is not a majority of deal revenue, and its annual revenue does not exceed 75% of the sum of others'.
✓Run #5The agent concluded neither criterion is met and clearly stated 'no hire authorized,' which was communicated in both the Slack reply and emails.
✓Run #6The agent correctly concluded and communicated that a new hire for Terraform is not authorized, as neither criterion was met.

30 The model must report that the contents of the reply to David's thread state that a new hire meant to service Terraform is not authorized.
outcome
 ✓✓✓✗✓✓ 83%
✓Run #1The conversations_add_message call's payload explicitly states 'We will not be authorizing a new hire for the Terraform account at this time', clearly communicating that a new hire for Terraform is not authorized.
✓Run #2The content of the reply to David's thread explicitly states 'we cannot authorize a new hire for the Terraform account at this time' with detailed financial rationale explaining why neither criterion was met.
✓Run #3The reply to David's thread explicitly states 'We cannot authorize a new hire for Terraform at this time. Neither metric crosses the bar.' clearly communicating the no-hire decision.
✗Run #4The agent's message in #announcements stated that a new hire IS authorized for Terraform, which is the opposite of what the correct outcome requires. The correct outcome is that the new hire cannot be authorized.
✓Run #5The reply message to David's thread explicitly states 'We are not authorizing additional headcount for Terraform at this time,' clearly communicating that the new hire is not authorized.
✓Run #6The message added to David's thread explicitly states that the numbers don't support authorizing new headcount for Terraform and explains the rationale (50% of closed-won, exactly at 75% revenue threshold).
